subject: Ecology
title: Parasitism
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Parasitism.

hook.text: A tapeworm in your intestine takes nutrients and gives absolutely nothing back.

buildup.text: Parasites benefit at their host's expense—sometimes slowly, sometimes fatally.

discovery.text: Nearly half of all known species are parasites. They dominate life's family tree.

twist.text: Toxoplasma makes infected mice fearless around cats—guiding the parasite to its next host.

climax.text: Parasites shape evolution, drive population cycles, and structure entire communities.

punchline.text: Half of all species make a living by stealing.

quiz.question: How does Toxoplasma affect infected mice?
